verses.text: Amaziah reigns and does right, but 'not with a perfect heart.' He executes his father's murderers yet spares their children, obeying the law that each die for his own sin. Half-hearted obedience is still flawed obedience. God weighs not only what we do but the heart that does it.

Amaziah reigns and does right, but 'not with a perfect heart.' He executes his father's murderers yet spares their children, obeying the law that each die for his own sin. Half-hearted obedience is still flawed obedience. God weighs not only what we do but the heart that does it.

Amaziah reigns and does right, but 'not with a perfect heart.' He executes his father's murderers yet spares their children, obeying the law that each die for his own sin. Half-hearted obedience is still flawed obedience. God weighs not only what we do but the heart that does it.

Amaziah reigns and does right, but 'not with a perfect heart.' He executes his father's murderers yet spares their children, obeying the law that each die for his own sin. Half-hearted obedience is still flawed obedience. God weighs not only what we do but the heart that does it.

Amaziah hires a hundred thousand soldiers from Israel, but a man of God warns him not to take them, 'for the LORD is not with Israel.' Alliances with the godless forfeit God's help. Better a small force with God than a great one without Him.

Amaziah hires a hundred thousand soldiers from Israel, but a man of God warns him not to take them, 'for the LORD is not with Israel.' Alliances with the godless forfeit God's help. Better a small force with God than a great one without Him.

Anxious over the hundred talents already paid, Amaziah hears, 'the LORD is able to give thee much more,' and sends the hired men home, though they leave in fierce anger. Obedience may cost money and provoke offence. Trusting God's bounty, he lets the silver go and keeps his integrity.

Anxious over the hundred talents already paid, Amaziah hears, 'the LORD is able to give thee much more,' and sends the hired men home, though they leave in fierce anger. Obedience may cost money and provoke offence. Trusting God's bounty, he lets the silver go and keeps his integrity.

Amaziah wins a great victory over Edom, but the dismissed Israelites, enraged, raid the cities of Judah on their way home. Obedience is honoured, yet trouble still follows. God's blessing does not always spare us every consequence, but it carries us through them.

Amaziah wins a great victory over Edom, but the dismissed Israelites, enraged, raid the cities of Judah on their way home. Obedience is honoured, yet trouble still follows. God's blessing does not always spare us every consequence, but it carries us through them.

Foolishly, Amaziah brings home the gods of conquered Edom and worships them; when a prophet rebukes him, he threatens the man, who warns, 'God hath determined to destroy thee.' To bow to the gods of those he defeated is madness. Pride that silences God's word seals its own ruin.

Foolishly, Amaziah brings home the gods of conquered Edom and worships them; when a prophet rebukes him, he threatens the man, who warns, 'God hath determined to destroy thee.' To bow to the gods of those he defeated is madness. Pride that silences God's word seals its own ruin.

Foolishly, Amaziah brings home the gods of conquered Edom and worships them; when a prophet rebukes him, he threatens the man, who warns, 'God hath determined to destroy thee.' To bow to the gods of those he defeated is madness. Pride that silences God's word seals its own ruin.

Proud after victory, Amaziah challenges Joash of Israel, who answers with the parable of the thistle that demanded marriage with the cedar: 'tarry at home; why meddle to thy hurt?' Success has puffed him up. Pride courts disaster, deaf to the counsel that would spare it.

Proud after victory, Amaziah challenges Joash of Israel, who answers with the parable of the thistle that demanded marriage with the cedar: 'tarry at home; why meddle to thy hurt?' Success has puffed him up. Pride courts disaster, deaf to the counsel that would spare it.

Amaziah will not listen, for it was 'of God' to deliver him up for his idolatry; he is routed at Beth-shemesh, Jerusalem's wall is broken down, and the temple plundered. God uses defeat to humble the proud idolater. Sin invites the very ruin that warnings could not make us fear.

Amaziah will not listen, for it was 'of God' to deliver him up for his idolatry; he is routed at Beth-shemesh, Jerusalem's wall is broken down, and the temple plundered. God uses defeat to humble the proud idolater. Sin invites the very ruin that warnings could not make us fear.

Amaziah outlives Joash, but after he turned from the LORD a conspiracy forms against him; he flees to Lachish and is killed there. The man who forsook God dies by his own people's hands. Turning from the LORD removes the only true safety a life can have.
